heading: Multiyear procurement authority for domestically processed critical minerals
label: 98e–2 Multiyear procurement authority for domestically processed critical minerals
notes: References in TextThe Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act, referred to in subsec. (b), is , as added , , , which is classified generally to this subchapter. children.heading: Authority for multiyear procurement
children.label: a Authority for multiyear procurement
children.text: Subject to and from amounts made available by discretionary appropriations Acts from the National Defense Stockpile Transaction Fund (as established under section 9(a) of the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act ()) after , the Secretary of Defense may enter into one or more multiyear contracts for the procurement of critical minerals that are processed in the United States by domestic sources.section 3501 of title 1050 U.S.C. 98h(a)December 22, 2023

children.heading: Application of Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act
children.label: b Application of Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act
children.text: A multiyear contract entered into under this section shall be deemed to be an acquisition under the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 98

children.heading: Authority for advance procurement
children.label: c Authority for advance procurement
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may enter into one or more contracts, beginning in fiscal year 2024, for advance procurement associated with the domestically processed critical minerals for which authorization to enter into a multiyear procurement contract is provided under subsection (a).

children.heading: Condition for out-year contract payments
children.label: d Condition for out-year contract payments
children.text: A contract entered into under subsection (a) shall provide that any obligation of the United States to make a payment under the contract for a fiscal year after fiscal year 2024 is subject to the availability of appropriations or funds for that purpose for such later fiscal year.
The term “critical mineral” means a mineral determined to be a strategic and critical material under section 3(a) of the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act ().50 U.S.C. 98b(a)

The term “processed” means the processing or recycling of a critical mineral or magnet, including the separation, reduction, metallization, alloying, milling, pressing, strip casting, and sintering of a critical mineral.
The term “domestic source” has the meaning given that term in .section 4552 of this title
